title: Area of Regular Polygons
why: A regular polygon can be split into equal triangles, so you find one triangle's area and multiply by how many triangles there are. That runs on triangle area (base × height ÷ 2), multiplication facts, and adding equal areas. Warm those up and a hexagon is just six triangles.

diagnostic.q: A triangle has base 4 and height 3. Its area is base × height ÷ 2 = ?
Six triangles each have area 4. What is the total area?

learn.heading: Split it into equal triangles
learn.intro: A regular polygon has all sides and angles equal. Draw lines from the center to each corner and it breaks into equal triangles. Find one triangle's area, then multiply by the number of triangles (or add them up — same thing).
examples: Hexagon: 6 equal triangles. If one triangle is 5 sq units, the hexagon is 6 × 5 = 30 square units.
Triangle area: base × height ÷ 2. Base 6, height 4 → 6 × 4 = 24, half of 24 = 12.

tiers.intro: Find one triangle, then count copies.

items.q: A. A triangle has base 4 and height 2. Find base × height: 4 × 2 = ___?
B. Take half of 8 to get one triangle's area: ___?
C. There are 3 of those triangles. Total = 3 × 4 = ___?

tiers.intro: Multiply one triangle by the number of triangles.

items.q: A. One triangle is 5 sq units. A pentagon has 5 of them. Total = 5 × 5 = ___?
B. One triangle is 6 sq units. A hexagon has 6 of them. Total = 6 × 6 = ___?

tiers.intro: Stretch: build one triangle, then the whole polygon.

items.q: A. A triangle has base 6 and height 4. Its area is base × height ÷ 2 = ___?
B. A hexagon is made of 6 such triangles. Total = 6 × 12 = ___?

exit.q: 1. A triangle has base 5 and height 4. Find base × height ÷ 2: ___?
2. Five equal triangles each have area 10. What is the total area?
